title: Project Wrap-Up & Gallery
concept: Testing the drawing pipeline with custom prompt variations. 'Mini Project: stable diffusion drawer is complete!'.

exercises.prompt: What do we do with the denoised latent vector to create a visible image?

options.text: We send it to the decoder.

exercises.explain: The denoised latent vector is passed through the decoder to create the final visible image.

cards.title: Testing Your Drawing Pipeline
cards.body: In this lesson, we'll learn how to test your artwork by changing prompts. Let's see how different ideas change your creations!

cards.title: Custom Prompt Variations
cards.body: Changing the words you use in your prompts can lead to different artistic results in your drawings.

cards.title: Like a Recipe
cards.body: Think of your prompt as a recipe. Changing the ingredients (words) creates a different dish (drawing) each time!

cards.title: Example Prompts
cards.body: Instead of just 'a cat', try 'a fluffy cat lounging on a sunny window sill' to see more unique results.

cards.title: Myth: More Words Always Help
cards.body: Using more words in prompts doesn't always guarantee better images. Sometimes, less is more!

cards.title: See the Differences
cards.body: Compare the results from different prompts.

left.text: Creates images of bright, warm colors.

right.text: Adds stars and deeper colors in the sky.

exercises.prompt: What happens when you change the words in your drawing prompts?
exercises.explain: Changing the words in your prompts can lead to a wide variety of images, showcasing the creativity of your drawings.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using longer prompts will always improve your artwork.
exercises.explain: While sometimes longer prompts can provide clarity, they do not always improve the quality of the artwork.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Changing the words in your prompts can lead to different __________ in your drawings.
exercises.sentence: Changing the words in your prompts can lead to different results in your drawings.
exercises.explain: The correct word, 'results', emphasizes how different prompts create distinct images.

exercises.prompt: Which of these prompts will likely create the most unique image?

options.text: A dog playing in a park
A dog wearing a superhero cape flying over the city

exercises.explain: The prompt with more creative details, 'A dog wearing a superhero cape flying over the city', invites richer artistic interpretations.

exercises.prompt: What is the best way to test your drawing pipeline after completing your mini-project?

options.text: Only use the same prompt every time.
Ask someone else to do it for you.

exercises.explain: Experimenting with different prompt variations allows you to explore the full potential of your drawing pipeline. Mini Project: stable diffusion drawer is complete!
